input.command:
cd /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ; git add docs/progress.md && git commit -m "docs: progress ledger — #758 W3 row + snapshot count

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push 2>&1 | tail -1 && gh pr create --title 'feat(lineage): OpenLineage emission — RunEvents + DQ facets, dark by default (G-d)' --milestone 'v1.1 Week 3 — Azure wind-down + local-first posture' --body 'Second G-d build issue (#596 design → ADR 0034 decision 3a): DataQ now emits OpenLineage RunEvents for every suite run — one vendor-neutral stream that reaches Marquez, DataHub (native OL HTTP receiver), or Kafka with zero per-catalog code.

## What
- **`app/lineage/`** — `emitter.py`: env-gated cached client (**dark by default**: none of `OPENLINEAGE_URL` / `OPENLINEAGE__TRANSPORT__TYPE` / `OPENLINEAGE_CONFIG` set, or `OPENLINEAGE_DISABLED` → no client, no package import, zero emission — the library'\''s own console-default never activates; gating mirrors the `otel.py` seam) + pure event builders; `dispatch.py`: fail-open choke point (mirrors `alerting/dispatch` — never raises, returns before any DB access when dark).
- **Hooked at the `run_suite` task** (documented deviation from "hooks in run_service"): brackets `_run_suite` with START before / COMPLETE·FAIL·ABORT after — one place covering execute/skip/early-fail paths, guaranteed 1:1 START+terminal per runId, `run_service`'\''s FakeSession contract untouched.
- **Facets:** `DataQualityAssertionsDatasetFacet` per (check, result) — skip omitted, operational `error` → `success=False`, severity `warn`→warn / `fail`·`critical`→error; `DataQualityMetricsInputDatasetFacet` `rowCount` from `observed_value["row_count"]` (**not** `metric_value`, which is the deviation %); FAIL carries `ErrorMessageRunFacet` with the classified `failure_reason` only. Dataset identity = the #757 `assets` row byte-for-byte.
- **PII discipline:** `sample_failures` is never read anywhere in the package; `observed_value`/`expected_value` deliberately excluded from assertion fields (GX can put failing column values in `observed_value`); dedicated serialization test asserts a planted sentinel never appears in a full event.
- **Dependency:** `openlineage-python==1.51.0` (Apache-2.0, rule-40 clean); transitive deps pip-audit clean; mypy via the existing `follow_imports=skip` block (no typecheck-subset change).

## Verification
- Gate: Black · Ruff · mypy (app+tests) · Bandit · **1700 passed / 98.32% cov** · pip-audit clean.
- **E2E over the real HTTP transport**: a real dev-DB run emitted through the full path (env gate → client → facets → HTTP POST) into a local OL receiver — START+COMPLETE captured, job `dataq/<suite>`, dataset `snowflake://acme-analytics / ANALYTICS.PUBLIC.ORDERS` (org-form account normalization live), assertions facet correct, **no sample values in raw payloads**.
- **Marquez read-back deferred to #762** (its compose stack lands Marquez in-repo): local Docker image pulls are wedged machine-wide (engine restart didn'\''t clear it — even a 3MB alpine pull hangs; host-level, unrelated to this change). The dev DB was also migrated to head en route: assets backfill live (3 assets; 1 demo suite fail-soft-skipped with a clean log line).

## Acceptance criteria (#758)
- [x] START/COMPLETE/FAIL events with dataset naming matching the #757 resolver byte-for-byte
- [x] Assertions + metrics facets from real result rows; no sample rows in events
- [x] Off-by-default (zero queries + zero network when unconfigured — `_ExplodingSession` proof); emit failure cannot fail a run
- [x] E2E verified over real HTTP (local OL receiver; Marquez consumer read-back rides #762''s compose work — noted above)

Closes #758

content.text: (Re-invocation of /code-review — the skill instructions were previously loaded; the arguments or dynamic output below are new.)

Review target: `765`

`high effort → 3+5 angles × 6 candidates → 1-vote verify (recall-biased) → ≤10 findings`

You are reviewing for **recall** at high effort: catch every real bug a careful
reviewer would catch in one sitting. At this level, catching real bugs matters
more than avoiding false positives. Err on the side of surfacing.

## Phase 0 — Gather the diff

Run `git diff @{upstream}...HEAD` (or `git diff main...HEAD` / `git diff HEAD~1`
if there's no upstream) to get the unified diff under review. If there are
uncommitted changes, or the range diff is empty, also run `git diff HEAD` and
include the working-tree changes in scope — the review often runs before the
commit. If a PR number, branch name, or file path was passed as an argument,
review that target instead. Treat this diff as the review scope.

## Phase 1 — Find candidates (3 correctness angles + 3 cleanup angles + 1 altitude angle + 1 conventions angle, up to 6 each)

Run **8 independent finder angles** via the Agent tool. Each
surfaces **up to 6 candidate findings** with `file`, `line`, a one-line
`summary`, and a concrete `failure_scenario`.

### Angle A — line-by-line diff scan

Read every hunk in the diff, line by line. Then Read the enclosing function for
each hunk — bugs in unchanged lines of a touched function are in scope (the PR
re-exposes or fails to fix them). For every line ask: what input, state, timing,
or platform makes this line wrong? Look for inverted/wrong conditions,
off-by-one, null/undefined deref, missing `await`, falsy-zero checks,
wrong-variable copy-paste, error swallowed in catch, unescaped regex metachars.

### Angle B — removed-behavior auditor

For every line the diff DELETES or replaces, name the invariant or behavior it
enforced, then search the new code for where that invariant is re-established.
If you can't find it, that's a candidate: a removed guard, a dropped error
path, a narrowed validation, a deleted test that was covering a real case.

### Angle C — cross-file tracer

For each function the diff changes, find its callers (Grep for the symbol) and
check whether the change breaks any call site: a new precondition, a changed
return shape, a new exception, a timing/ordering dependency. Also check callees:
does a parallel change in the same PR make a call unsafe?

### Reuse

The angles above hunt for bugs; this one and the next two hunt for cleanup in
the changed code. Flag new code that re-implements something the codebase
already has — Grep shared/utility modules and files adjacent to the change,
and name the existing helper to call instead.

### Simplification

Flag unnecessary complexity the diff adds: redundant or derivable state,
copy-paste with slight variation, deep nesting, dead code left behind. Name
the simpler form that does the same job.

### Efficiency

Flag wasted work the diff introduces: redundant computation or repeated I/O,
independent operations run sequentially, blocking work added to startup or
hot paths. Also flag long-lived objects built from closures or captured
environments — they keep the entire enclosing scope alive for the object's
lifetime (a memory leak when that scope holds large values); prefer a
class/struct that copies only the fields it needs. Name the cheaper
alternative.

### Altitude

Check that each change is implemented at the right depth, not as a fragile
bandaid. Special cases layered on shared infrastructure are a sign the fix
isn't deep enough — prefer generalizing the underlying mechanism over adding
special cases.

### Conventions (CLAUDE.md)

Find the CLAUDE.md files that govern the changed code: the user-level
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, the repo-root CLAUDE.md, plus any CLAUDE.md or
CLAUDE.local.md in a directory that is an ancestor of a changed file (a
directory's CLAUDE.md only applies to files at or below it). Read each one
that exists, then check the diff for clear violations of the rules they state.

Only flag a violation when you can quote the exact rule and the exact line
that breaks it — no style preferences, no vague "spirit of the doc"
inferences. In the finding, name the CLAUDE.md path and quote the rule so the
report can cite it. If no CLAUDE.md applies, return nothing for this angle.

Cleanup, altitude, and conventions candidates use the same
`file`/`line`/`summary` shape; in `failure_scenario`, state the concrete
cost (what is duplicated, wasted, harder to maintain, or which CLAUDE.md rule
is broken) instead of a crash. Correctness bugs always outrank cleanup,
altitude, and conventions findings when the output cap forces a cut.

Pass every candidate with a nameable failure scenario through — finders that
silently drop half-believed candidates bypass the verify step and are the
dominant cause of misses.

## Phase 2 — Verify (1-vote, recall-biased)

Dedup near-duplicates (same defect, same location, same reason → keep one). For
each remaining candidate, run **one verifier** via the Agent tool:
give it the diff, the relevant file(s), and the candidate; it returns exactly
one of **CONFIRMED / PLAUSIBLE / REFUTED**.

**PLAUSIBLE by default** — do not refute a candidate for being "speculative" or
"depends on runtime state" when the state is realistic: concurrency races,
nil/undefined on a rare-but-reachable path (error handler, cold cache, missing
optional field), falsy-zero treated as missing, off-by-one on a boundary the
code does not exclude, retry storms / partial failures, regex/allowlist that
lost an anchor. These are PLAUSIBLE.

**REFUTED** only when constructible from the code: factually wrong (quote the
actual line); provably impossible (type/constant/invariant — show it); already
handled in this diff (cite the guard); or pure style with no observable effect.

Keep **CONFIRMED and PLAUSIBLE**. Drop REFUTED.

## Output

Return findings as a JSON array of at most 10 objects:

```json
[
  {
    "file": "path/to/file.ext",
    "line": 123,
    "summary": "one-sentence statement of the bug",
    "failure_scenario": "concrete inputs/state → wrong output/crash"
  }
]
```

Ranked most-severe first. If more than 10 survive, keep the 10 most
severe. If nothing survives verification, return `[]`.


## Posting to GitHub (--comment)

The `--comment` flag was passed. After producing the findings list, if the
review target is a GitHub PR, post each finding as an inline PR comment via
`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` (one call per finding;
include a suggestion block only when it fully fixes the issue). If that tool
is not available in this session, fall back to `gh api` (repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr}/comments)
or print the findings instead. If the target is not a PR, print the findings
to the terminal and note that `--comment` was ignored.

input.prompt:
You are one finder angle in a code review of PR #765 in /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ (branch feature/758-openlineage-emission vs main). Run `git diff main...HEAD`. Scope: new backend/app/lineage/ package (emitter.py env-gated OpenLineage client + event builders, dispatch.py fail-open choke point), hooks in backend/app/worker/tasks.py bracketing _run_suite, openlineage-python==1.51.0 in requirements.txt, pyproject mypy override, tests/lineage/ + a worker test, docs/progress.md.

ANGLE A — line-by-line scan of every hunk + the enclosing functions. Hunt specifically: the env gate (`is_emission_configured` — truthy parsing of OPENLINEAGE_DISABLED: what about "0"/"false" set explicitly with a URL — is disabled=false handled right? empty-string URL?); the cached singleton (double-checked locking correctness; does a construction failure cache None FOREVER so a config fix mid-process never recovers — is that intended/documented? thread safety of reset); event builders (timezone handling of started_at/finished_at naive vs aware; run.status values → eventType mapping completeness — what does status "queued"/"running" map to if the terminal hook fires on a still-running row? cancelled → ABORT; anything unmapped → crash or silent default?); assertions facet (check/result zip by check_id lookup — results whose check was deleted mid-run? checks with no result?); the START hook placement in tasks.py (START emitted BEFORE _run_suite — but _run_suite may find run already cancelled/missing: does terminal still fire? is a START-without-terminal possible, or terminal-without-START?); dispatch (session.get(Run) None → ?; asset None path; does emit_run_lineage_terminal query Results/Checks efficiently); serialization of Decimal metric_value / UUID / datetime in facets (openlineage serde handles them?); the httpx/requests transport timeout — can a slow OL endpoint stall the worker task (fail-open catches exceptions but not slowness — is there a timeout configured or documented)?

Return up to 6 candidates as a JSON array: [{"file","line","summary","failure_scenario"}]. Final message = ONLY the JSON array.

You are one finder angle in a code review of PR #765 in /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ (branch feature/758-openlineage-emission vs main). Run `git diff main...HEAD`.

ANGLE B — removed-behavior auditor. The diff is mostly additive; focus on replaced/touched lines: (1) backend/app/worker/tasks.py `run_suite` task — the try/finally around _run_suite + alert_dispatch: did the insertion of lineage calls change exception propagation, the return value, or the finally-close ordering? If emit_run_lineage_start runs BEFORE _run_suite and raises unexpectedly at import level (module import errors are not runtime exceptions inside the function — is the import at module top?), does the task now fail where it didn't? (2) pyproject.toml mypy override block — did adding openlineage.* to follow_imports=skip accidentally widen/narrow existing entries or change other overrides? Show the before/after. (3) requirements.txt — any version changed beyond the addition? (4) tests/worker/test_tasks.py — were existing assertions weakened to accommodate the new hooks (e.g. a test that asserted exact call order/counts on alert_dispatch)? (5) docs/progress.md — count integrity: W3 header says 12/15 — count the actual rows/checkmarks in the post-diff table; Snapshot open-issues 73 vs GitHub reality at merge.

Return up to 6 candidates as JSON: [{"file","line","summary","failure_scenario"}]. Final message = ONLY the JSON array.

You are one finder angle in a code review of PR #765 in /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ (branch feature/758-openlineage-emission vs main). Run `git diff main...HEAD`.

ANGLE C — cross-file tracer. (1) The `run_suite` Celery task is the ONLY hook site — grep for other paths that drive runs to terminal without going through that task: the cancel endpoint (run cancel — does a cancelled run get its ABORT event? who flips status to cancelled?), the stuck-run reaper (#458 — reaps runs the worker lost: those get no terminal OL event → START-without-terminal in the consumer; real?), the dryrun_service (does it create Run rows? should it emit? probably not — verify it doesn't go through run_suite), retention sweep (touches results after the fact — no impact?). For each, decide if a lineage event gap/mismatch results and how a consumer (Marquez) would see it (dangling RUNNING run). (2) The dispatch module queries Run/Suite/Asset/Check/Result — check the session context in the task (get_session() — same session used by _run_suite after it committed; any expired-object/identity-map staleness where run.status read by the terminal emitter could be stale?). (3) celery beat / other tasks that call _run_suite or execute_run directly (grep) — bypass the hooks? (4) The mypy pyproject override + requirements-typecheck sync guard (typecheck-deps-sync pre-commit + CI): run/read the sync check to confirm it passes with the new dep arrangement. (5) The `worker -B` embedded beat + fork behavior: emitter caches a client with a lock at module level — any fork-safety concern (celery prefork workers: is the client constructed pre-fork in the parent, then shared post-fork — requests Session/httpx connection pools across forks)? Compare how otel/logging handled fork-safety (createLock idempotence #405, register_at_fork comments).

Return up to 6 candidates as JSON: [{"file","line","summary","failure_scenario"}]. Final message = ONLY the JSON array.

You are one finder angle in a code review of PR #765 in /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ (branch feature/758-openlineage-emission vs main). Run `git diff main...HEAD`.

ANGLE REUSE — new code re-implementing what exists: does emitter.py re-derive severity ordering instead of using db.models SEVERITY_RANK/FAILING_TIERS/worst_severity (#657 canonical helpers)? Does the singleton+lock+reset pattern duplicate app/alerting/registry.py's shape where a shared helper could serve (or is duplication the convention — check how many such singletons exist)? Does dispatch.py re-implement run/suite/asset loading that a shared loader already provides? Do the new tests duplicate fixture builders from tests/alerting or tests/services (fake sessions, run/suite factories) instead of reusing? Does the env-gate re-implement settings parsing that config.py or otel.py already centralize (should is_emission_configured live beside the otel gate)? Name the existing helper for each.

Return up to 6 candidates as JSON: [{"file","line","summary","failure_scenario"}]. Only real duplication with drift cost. Final message = ONLY the JSON array.

You are one finder angle in a code review of PR #765 in /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ (branch feature/758-openlineage-emission vs main). Run `git diff main...HEAD`.

ANGLE SIMPLIFICATION — read backend/app/lineage/emitter.py + dispatch.py in full. Flag: dead branches (checks for states that can't occur), redundant lazy imports (same module imported lazily in 5+ functions — would one module-level lazy accessor be simpler? weigh against the dark-path-import-cost rationale), over-general parameters never varied by callers, the START/terminal dispatch pair sharing 90% code (loader + gate + fail-open) that could be one helper, test bloat (multiple tests asserting the same rule), docstrings restating ADR content at length. Name the simpler form; skip pure style.

Return up to 6 candidates as JSON: [{"file","line","summary","failure_scenario"}]. Final message = ONLY the JSON array.

You are one finder angle in a code review of PR #765 in /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ (branch feature/758-openlineage-emission vs main). Run `git diff main...HEAD`.

ANGLE EFFICIENCY — wasted work in the new lineage path: (1) is_emission_configured / env reads on EVERY run when dark (os.environ lookups are cheap — only flag if something heavier happens on the dark path: imports, locks, queries); (2) the terminal dispatch loading Checks+Results — duplicate queries when _run_suite already had them in memory (the hook is post-commit at the task layer, so a re-query per run: real cost is small — flag only with a cheaper alternative that doesn't break the choke-point design); (3) synchronous HTTP emit inside the worker task — latency added per run when configured (transport timeout? async transport option the client offers?); (4) two emit calls (START before, terminal after) = two HTTP round-trips per run — fine, but is the client reconstructed per call or cached? For each real cost name the cheaper alternative; if trivially cheap at single-tenant scale, do NOT flag.

Return up to 6 candidates as JSON: [{"file","line","summary","failure_scenario"}]. Final message = ONLY the JSON array.

You are one finder angle in a code review of PR #765 in /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ (branch feature/758-openlineage-emission vs main). Run `git diff main...HEAD`.

ANGLE ALTITUDE — right depth? (1) The hook lives in the run_suite Celery task — is that the single run-terminal choke point in this codebase, or do other paths terminate runs (cancel endpoint, stuck-run reaper #458) that now silently bypass lineage: is the RIGHT depth a status-transition seam (e.g. a run_state module) rather than the task? Weigh against the PR's documented rationale. (2) The env gate reads os.environ directly instead of Settings — otel.py gates via Settings fields; is a second gating idiom a fragmentation cost, or justified because the OL client owns those vars? (3) severity mapping (warn→warn, fail/critical→error) hardcoded in the emitter — should it derive from db.models severity constants so a future tier addition flows through? (4) job name = suite.name — suites are renameable and names may not be unique across envs: is run history in a consumer keyed stably (job identity churn on rename) — should job name use a stable id or include env? Does the design doc/ADR pin this? (5) The Marquez-read-back deferral to #762 — is anything in THIS PR's shape (transport config, event contract) likely to need rework when Marquez lands, or is the contract spec-pinned?

Return up to 6 candidates as JSON: [{"file","line","summary","failure_scenario"}]. Final message = ONLY the JSON array.

You are one finder angle in a code review of PR #765 in /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ (branch feature/758-openlineage-emission vs main). Run `git diff main...HEAD`.

ANGLE CONVENTIONS — read /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/CLAUDE.md (§6 working agreements, §10, §11, dependency rules) + CONTRIBUTING.md. Flag CLEAR violations only (quote rule + line): license rule 40 (openlineage-python Apache-2.0 — verify the requirements.txt comment states it; transitive deps?); "Python deps have one source of truth: backend/requirements.txt" — was requirements-typecheck.txt/typecheck-deps-sync handled correctly for the mypy override approach (run the sync check if runnable); pin-exact convention; PII redaction rules (emitter logs — do any log lines include event payloads/URLs with potential creds? OPENLINEAGE_URL could carry an api key query param — does any log line print it? core/logging redactor covers secret query params — verify the emitter's warning logs route through structlog); conventional commits on all three commits; one-functionality-per-commit; docs updated (progress.md yes; anything else required — env-var reference docs (#468's deployment guide / env reference) documents all env vars: should OPENLINEAGE_* be added there per the prod-docs gate #464?).

Return up to 6 candidates as JSON: [{"file","line","summary","failure_scenario" (quote rule + file)}]. If none, []. Final message = ONLY the JSON array.

content.text: All eight review finders are running against PR #765 — this round's high-value hunting grounds are called out explicitly: the cancel-endpoint and stuck-run-reaper paths that bypass the task-layer hook (potential START-without-terminal in consumers), fork-safety of the cached client under Celery prefork, the permanently-cached-None construction failure, transport timeout stalling the worker, and whether `OPENLINEAGE_URL` with an API-key query param could leak into logs. I'll verify, post, and fix when they report.
